description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone with a rectangular base featuring a woodgrain finish and a black plastic handset resting on top. The front of the base displays a numeric keypad with white numbers on black buttons, arranged in a standard telephone layout. The handset is cradled on the left side of the base, with a visible cord connecting it to the main unit. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, typical of telephones found in homes or offices from the late 20th century. The materials appear to be primarily plastic with a faux wood veneer, and the buttons are slightly raised for tactile feedback. The phone is designed to sit on a flat surface and is not wall-mounted.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a woodgrain base, black handset, and numeric keypad. The handset rests on a cradle and is connected to the base by a cord.
description_view_0: The front-left view shows the black handset resting on the woodgrain base, with the numeric keypad clearly visible.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the slim profile of the phone, with the handset and base aligned vertically.
description_view_2: The front-right view again highlights the keypad and handset, mirroring the opposite side but from a different angle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the thin edge of the phone, similar to view 1, with minimal detail visible.
